compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why
After dropping the bot + FFO, EVERY prose reference to autosubmit, the auto-lander, the `land`/`land-status` command, `lander_down`, or the FFO Gerrit workflow is inaccurate. This is a comprehensive prose sweep of the live landing guidance.

## What — grep-driven prose sweep
Rewrite the landing narrative to the new model in the live-guidance files, and neutralize the historical decision records:
- Rewritten to the new model: CLAUDE.md (Git-workflow land section), CONTRIBUTING.md (§2e land flow, feature-branch §4c/§4e land pointers, troubleshooting), README.md (landing pointer), docs/your-first-change.md (walkthrough land step), docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md (drop the retired autolander SSM-token row).
- Banner-only (historical prose left intact): ADR 0040 + ADR 0042 get a "Superseded by ADR 0047" banner.
- Light pointer-note (submit type is now Rebase-If-Necessary; prior-model logic left for a separate review): ADR 0025, ADR 0041.

## New model documented everywhere
Land via a plain Gerrit **Submit** once **LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1** (and no unresolved comments); `main` is **Rebase-If-Necessary**, so Gerrit rebases onto the tip and submits server-side/atomically — no pre-rebase in the common case. On a **textual** conflict only: `git fetch && git rebase origin/main`, re-push, re-Submit. **No bot, no Autosubmit label, no land/land-status command, no lander_down fallback.** Post-merge `main` CI (test.yml on push) + manual revert is the bounded-risk safety net.

## Scope notes (division with siblings)
- **infra/** autolander references are sibling **bcf0**; the `land`/`land-status` command was a standalone argparse `main` in infra/autolander/land.py (removed by **d0e5**), NOT a rebar CLI subcommand or MCP tool — so there is no `--help`/reference-doc regeneration for it.
- **CLAUDE.local.md** is git-ignored, not a committed repo file, and already matches the new model — no repo edit.
- **Expected NON-swept residuals** of the broad terms `auto-lander`/`autolander` and `FFO`/`fast-forward-only`: (a) the two superseded ADRs 0040/0042 (historical prose under a supersession banner) + ADR 0047 itself; (b) the pointer-noted ADRs 0025/0041; (c) the ADR-0047 filename `0047-retire-autolander-rebase-if-necessary.md` inside hyperlinks (contains "autolander"); (d) the GitHub-**mirror** "fast-forward only" in docs/adr/0010 + docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md, which is the replication push refspec (ADR-0010 one-way-door), a DIFFERENT concept from the `main` submit type and correctly left. These are all expected and out of scope.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Retired-model terms ABSENT from the live-guidance files (machine-checkable, unambiguous terms only): `git grep -nE "autosubmit|Autosubmit|lander_down|land-status|land-contract|land <change>|rebase[- ]on[- ]behalf|--wait" -- CLAUDE.md CONTRIBUTING.md README.md docs/your-first-change.md docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` returns NOTHING. (The broad terms `auto-lander`/`FFO`/`fast-forward-only` are intentionally NOT in this grep because they legitimately survive in the historical/pointer ADRs, the ADR-0047 filename links, and the different-concept GitHub-mirror refspec — see Scope notes.)
- [ ] New model PRESENT: `for f in CLAUDE.md CONTRIBUTING.md README.md docs/your-first-change.md; do grep -q "Rebase-If-Necessary" "$f" || echo "MISSING $f"; done` prints nothing (all four live-guidance files, README.md included).
- [ ] Retired autolander SSM-token row removed from docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md: `grep -c "autolander-gerrit-token" docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` returns 0.
- [ ] Historical ADRs neutralized: `grep -l "Superseded by \[ADR 0047\]" docs/adr/0040-*.md docs/adr/0042-*.md` lists both, and `grep -l "Rebase-If-Necessary" docs/adr/0025-*.md docs/adr/0041-*.md` lists both (pointer-notes).
- [ ] docs-index / dead-link checker green: `python scripts/check_docs_index.py --check` exits 0.
- [ ] make lint && make typecheck && make test green.
compiled_state.title: Rewrite landing docs for the Submit-when-green / Gerrit-rebases model
